title: noronhia divaricata
intro: Noronhia divaricata, also known as the Madagascar olive, is a plant species native to Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Noronhia divaricata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
